{"result":"Method: crm.lead.list
Module: crm
Category: list
Scope: crm
Description: The method crm.lead.list returns a list of leads based on the filter. It is an implementation of the list method for leads.
URL: https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/crm/leads/crm-lead-list.html
Parameters:
- select: (array) The array contains a list of fields to select. Use masks: '*' to select all fields (excluding custom and multiple fields), 'UF_*' to select all custom fields (excluding multiple fields). To select multiple fields, specify the required ones explicitly. [required: no]
- filter: (object) An object for filtering the selected leads in the format {'field_1': 'value_1', ... 'field_N': 'value_N'}. Possible values for field correspond to lead fields. The key can have prefixes like >=, >, etc. to clarify filter behavior. [required: no]
Returns:
- result: (array) An array of leads matching the filter criteria with the selected fields.
Examples:
Example 1:
```php
try {\n    $leadsResult = getCRMScope()->lead()->list($order, $filter, $select, $startItem);\n    foreach ($leadsResult->getLeads() as $lead) {\n        print('ID: {$lead->ID}, TITLE: {$lead->TITLE}, NAME: {$lead->NAME}, BIRTHDATE: ' . ($lead->BIRTHDATE ? $lead->BIRTHDATE->format(DATE_ATOM) : 'N/A') . '\\n');\n    }\n} catch (Throwable $e) {\n    print('Error: ' . $e->getMessage());\n}
```

Example 2:
```js
BX24.callMethod(\n  'crm.lead.list',\n  {\n    select: ['*', 'UF_*'],\n    start: 50,\n    filter: {\n        '=OPPORTUNITY': 15000\n    },\n    order: {\n        STATUS_ID: 'ASC'\n    }\n  },\n  (result) => {\n    if(result.error()) {\n      console.error(result.error());\n      return;\n    }\n    console.info(result.data());\n  }\n);
```

Example 3:
```php
require_once('crest.php');\n$result = CRest::call(\n    'crm.lead.list',\n    [\n        'select' => ['*', 'UF_*'],\n        'start' => 50,\n        'filter' => [\n            '=OPPORTUNITY' => 15000\n        ],\n        'order' => [\n            'STATUS_ID' => 'ASC'\n        ]\n    ]\n);\necho '';\nprint_r($result);\necho '';
```

Example 4:
```js
BX24.callMethod(\n    'crm.lead.list',\n    {\n        order: { 'STATUS_ID': 'ASC' },\n        filter: { '>OPPORTUNITY': 0, '!STATUS_ID': 'CONVERTED' },\n        select: [ 'ID', 'TITLE', 'STATUS_ID', 'OPPORTUNITY', 'CURRENCY_ID' ]\n    },\n    (result) => {\n        if(result.error()) {\n            console.error(result.error());\n        } else {\n            console.dir(result.data());\n            if (result.more()) {\n                result.next();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n);
```

Example 5:
```js
BX24.callMethod(\n    'crm.lead.list',\n    {\n        filter: { 'PHONE': '555888' },\n        select: [ 'ID', 'TITLE' ]\n    },\n    (result) => {\n      if(result.error()) {\n        console.error(result.error());\n      } else {\n        console.dir(result.data());\n        if (result.more()) {\n          result.next();\n        }\n      }\n    }\n);
```"}

{"result":"Method: crm.lead.update
Module: crm
Category: update
Scope: crm
Description: The method crm.lead.update updates an existing lead.
URL: https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/crm/leads/crm-lead-update.html
Parameters:
- id: (integer) Lead identifier. The identifier can be obtained using the methods crm.lead.list or crm.lead.add [required: yes]
- fields: (object) Object format: { field_1: value_1, field_2: value_2, ..., field_n: value_n, } where: - field_n — field name - value_n — new field value The list of available fields is described below. An incorrect field in fields will be ignored. Only those fields that need to be changed should be passed in fields [required: no]
-   LEAD: Fields for updating a lead
-     - ADDRESS: (string) Lead address
-     - ADDRESS_2: (string) Second line of the address. In some countries, it is customary to split the address into 2 parts
-     - ADDRESS_CITY: (string) City
-     - ADDRESS_COUNTRY: (string) Country
-     - ADDRESS_COUNTRY_CODE: (string) Country code
-     - ADDRESS_POSTAL_CODE: (string) Postal code
-     - ADDRESS_PROVINCE: (string) State
-     - ADDRESS_REGION: (string) Region
-     - ASSIGNED_BY_ID: (user) Responsible person
-     - BIRTHDATE: (date) Date of birth
-     - COMMENTS: (string) Comments
-     - COMPANY_ID: (crm_company) Link the lead to a company
-     - COMPANY_TITLE: (string) Company name specified in the corresponding lead field. To link an existing company, pass its id in the COMPANY_ID field
-     - CONTACT_ID: (crm_contact) Link the lead to a contact
-     - CONTACT_IDS: (crm_contact) List of contacts linked to the lead. Contacts can be added or removed using the group of methods crm.lead.contact.*
-     - CURRENCY_ID: (crm_currency) Currency identifier
-     - EMAIL: (crm_multifield) E-mail address. Multiple
-     - HONORIFIC: (crm_status) Salutation
-     - IM: (crm_multifield) Messenger. Multiple
-     - LINK: (crm_multifield) User ID linked through an open channel. Multiple
-     - LAST_NAME: (string) Last name
-     - NAME: (string) First name
-     - SECOND_NAME: (string) Middle name
-     - OPENED: (char) Indicator of lead availability for everyone. Acceptable values Y or N
-     - OPPORTUNITY: (double) Amount
-     - IS_MANUAL_OPPORTUNITY: (char) Indicator of manual calculation mode for the amount. Acceptable values Y or N
-     - ORIGINATOR_ID: (string) Identifier of the data source. Used only for linking to an external source
-     - ORIGIN_ID: (string) Identifier of the item in the data source. Used only for linking to an external source
-     - PHONE: (crm_multifield) Phone. Multiple
-     - POST: (string) Position
-     - SOURCE_DESCRIPTION: (string) Source description
-     - SOURCE_ID: (crm_status) Source identifier. Default values: CALL, EMAIL, WEB, ADVERTISING, PARTNER, RECOMMENDATION, TRADE_SHOW, WEBFORM, CALLBACK, RC_GENERATOR, STORE, OTHER. The list of all possible identifiers from the directory can be obtained using the method crm.status.list with the filter filter[ENTITY_ID]=SOURCE
-     - STATUS_DESCRIPTION: (string) Additional information about the stage
-     - STATUS_ID: (crm_status) Identifier of the lead stage. Default stages: NEW, IN_PROCESS, PROCESSED, JUNK, CONVERTED. The list of all possible stages from the directory can be obtained using the method crm.status.list with the filter filter[ENTITY_ID]=STATUS
-     - TITLE: (string) Lead title
-     - UTM_CAMPAIGN: (string) Advertising campaign designation
-     - UTM_CONTENT: (string) Campaign content. For example, for contextual ads

-     - UF_...: (string) Custom fields. For example, UF_CRM_25534736. Depending on the account settings, leads may have a set of custom fields of specific types. To change file fields, it is recommended to use the method crm.item.update. To create, modify, or delete custom fields in leads, use the methods crm.lead.userfield.*

{"result":"Method: crm.status.list
Module: crm
Category: list
Scope: crm, user_brief
Description: The crm.status.list method returns a description of stages by the ENTITY_ID stage code for CRM objects, reflecting the current state of a CRM entity such as in progress, successfully completed, or unsuccessful.
URL: https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/tutorials/crm/how-to-get-lists/how-to-get-stages-with-semantics.html
Parameters:
- order: (object) Sorting order of the results, e.g., { SORT: 'ASC' } for ascending order by the SORT field. [required: no]
- filter: (object) Filter criteria to specify which stages to return, e.g., { ENTITY_ID: 'STATUS' } to get stages for leads. [required: no]
- select: (array) Array of fields to select in the response, e.g., ['STATUS_ID', 'NAME', 'EXTRA']. [required: no]
Returns:
- result: (array) Array of stage objects describing each stage with fields such as ID, ENTITY_ID, STATUS_ID, NAME, SORT, SYSTEM, COLOR, SEMANTICS, and EXTRA containing semantics and color.
- total: (integer) Total number of stages returned.
- time: (time) Information about the execution time of the request
Errors:
- 400: Access denied.
- 400: Invalid parameters.
- 500: INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- 500: ERROR_UNEXPECTED_ANSWER
- 503: QUERY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- 405: ERROR_BATCH_METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED
- 400: ERROR_BATCH_LENGTH_EXCEEDED
- 401: NO_AUTH_FOUND
- 400: INVALID_REQUEST
- 503: OVERLOAD_LIMIT
- 403: ACCESS_DENIED
- 403: INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- 404: ERROR_MANIFEST_IS_NOT_AVAILABLE
- 403: insufficient_scope
- 401: expired_token
- 403: user_access_error
Examples:
Example 1:
```curl
curl -X POST \\n     -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \\n     -H 'Accept: application/json' \\n     -d '{'order':{'SORT':'ASC'},'filter':{'ENTITY_ID':'DEAL_STAGE'}}' \\n     https://**put_your_bitrix24_address**/rest/**put_your_user_id_here**/**put_your_webhook_here**/crm.status.list
```

Example 2:
```curl
curl -X POST \\n-H 'Content-Type: application/json' \\n-H 'Accept: application/json' \\n-d '{'order':{'SORT':'ASC'},'filter':{'ENTITY_ID':'DEAL_STAGE'},'auth':'**put_access_token_here**'}' \\nhttps://**put_your_bitrix24_address**/rest/crm.status.list
```

Example 3:
```js
// callListMethod is recommended when you need to retrieve the entire set of list data and the volume of records is relatively small (up to about 1000 items). The method loads all data at once, which can lead to high memory load when working with large volumes.\\ntry {\\n  const response = await $b24.callListMethod(\\n    'crm.status.list',\\n    {\\n      order: { SORT: 'ASC' },\\n      filter: { ENTITY_ID: 'DEAL_STAGE' }\\n    }\\n  )\\n  const items = response.getData() || []\\n  for (const entity of items) { console.log('Entity:', entity) }\\n} catch (error) {\\n  console.error('Request failed', error)\\n}\\n// fetchListMethod is preferable when working with large datasets. The method implements iterative selection using a generator, allowing data to be processed in parts and efficiently using memory.\\ntry {\\n  const generator = $b24.fetchListMethod('crm.status.list', {\\n    order: { SORT: 'ASC' },\\n    filter: { ENTITY_ID: 'DEAL_STAGE' }\\n  }, 'ID')\\n  for await (const page of generator) {\\n    for (const entity of page) { console.log('Entity:', entity) }\\n  }\\n} catch (error) {\\n  console.error('Request failed', error)\\n}\\n// callMethod provides manual control over the pagination process through the start parameter. It is suitable for scenarios where precise control over request batches is required. However, it may be less efficient compared to fetchListMethod when dealing with large volumes of data.\\ntry {\\n  const response = await $b24.callMethod('crm.status.list', {\\n    order: { SORT: 'ASC' },\\n    filter: { ENTITY_ID: 'DEAL_STAGE' }\\n  }, 0)\\n  const result = response.getData().result || []\\n  for (const entity of result) { console.log('Entity:', entity) }\\n} catch (error) {\\n  console.error('Request failed', error)\\n}
```

Example 4:
```php
try {\\n    $response = $b24Service\\n        ->core\\n        ->call(\\n            'crm.status.list',\\n            [\\n                'order' => ['SORT' => 'ASC'],\\n                'filter' => ['ENTITY_ID' => 'DEAL_STAGE'],\\n            ]\\n        );\\n    $result = $response\\n        ->getResponseData()\\n        ->getResult();\\n    if ($result->error()) {\\n        error_log($result->error());\\n        echo 'Error: ' . $result->error();\\n    } else {\\n        echo 'Success: ' . print_r($result->data(), true);\\n    }\\n} catch (Throwable $e) {\\n    error_log($e->getMessage());\\n    echo 'Error fetching status list: ' . $e->getMessage();\\n}
```

Example 5:
```js
BX24.callMethod(\\n    'crm.status.list',\\n    {\\n        order: { SORT: 'ASC' },\\n        filter: { ENTITY_ID: 'DEAL_STAGE' }\\n    },\\n    function(result) {\\n        if(result.error())\\n            console.error(result.error());\\n        else\\n            console.dir(result.data());\\n    }\\n);
```

Example 6:
```php
require_once('crest.php');\\n$result = CRest::call(\\n    'crm.status.list',\\n    [\\n        'order' => [ 'SORT' => 'ASC' ],\\n        'filter' => [ 'ENTITY_ID' => 'DEAL_STAGE' ]\\n    ]\\n);\\necho '';\\nprint_r($result);\\necho '';
```

{"result":"1. "Event onCrmLeadUpdate" (event) - The ONCRMLEADUPDATE event triggers when a lead is updated in the CRM module.
2. "When updating a custom field onCrmLeadUserFieldUpdate" (event) - The event ONCRMLEADUSERFIELDUPDATE triggers when a custom field for leads is updated.
3. "Event onCrmContactUpdate" (event) - The onCrmContactUpdate event triggers when a contact is updated in the CRM.
4. "Event onCrmActivityUpdate" (event) - The onCrmActivityUpdate event is triggered when an activity is updated in the CRM timeline.
5. "Event onCrmLeadAdd" (event) - The ONCRMLEADADD event triggers when a new lead is created in the CRM. It sends data as a POST request containing information about the created lead and authorization details.
6. "Event for updating a custom type CRM object onCrmDynamicItemUpdate" (event) - The event triggers when an element of any custom object type in CRM is modified.
7. "Event onCrmInvoiceUpdate" (event) - The event is triggered when an invoice is updated.
8. "Event on Company Update onCrmCompanyUpdate" (event) - The event ONCRMCOMPANYUPDATE triggers when a company is updated in the CRM. It sends data about the updated company fields and authorization details as a POST request.
9. "Overview of Events When Working with Leads" (event) - Events allow applications to respond to changes in almost real-time by receiving notifications about the creation, update, or deletion of leads.
10. "Overview of Events When Working with Custom Lead Fields" (event) - Events allow applications to respond to changes in near real-time: receiving notifications about the creation, update, or deletion of custom lead fields.
11. "When Adding a Custom Field onCrmLeadUserFieldAdd" (event) - The event ONCRMLEADUSERFIELDADD triggers when a new custom field is added for leads in CRM.
12. "Event on User Field Change onCrmContactUserFieldUpdate" (event) - The event onCrmContactUserFieldUpdate is triggered when a user field is changed for contacts.
13. "Event onCrmProductSectionUpdate" (event) - The event onCrmProductSectionUpdate is triggered after the product section is modified.
14. "Event onCrmMeasureUpdate" (event) - The event onCrmMeasureUpdate is triggered after the unit of measurement is changed on the account.
15. "Event on Lead Deletion onCrmLeadDelete" (event) - The event ONCRMLEADDELETE triggers when a lead is deleted, providing data about the deleted lead and authorization details.
16. "Event on deal update of type \"Comment\" onCrmTimelineCommentUpdate" (event) - The event triggers on the update of a deal of type 'Comment' in the CRM timeline.
17. "Event onCrmProductUpdate" (event) - The event onCrmProductUpdate is triggered when a product is updated. It is still operational but has a more relevant alternative: CATALOG.PRODUCT.ON.UPDATE.
18. "Event on deal update onCrmDealUpdate" (event) - The event ONCRMDEALUPDATE triggers when a deal is updated and sends data about the updated deal fields and authorization information.
19. "When setting list values for the custom field onCrmLeadUserFieldSetEnumValues" (event) - The event ONCRMLEADUSERFIELDSETENUMVALUES triggers when setting list values for custom fields of leads.
20. "Event on user field change onCrmDealUserFieldUpdate" (event) - The event onCrmDealUserFieldUpdate triggers when a user field in a CRM deal is changed, providing details about the updated field and authorization information."}

messages.content:
{"result":"# Event onCrmLeadUpdate
URL: https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/crm/leads/events/on-crm-lead-update.html

## Description
The ONCRMLEADUPDATE event triggers when a lead is updated in the CRM module.

## Content
> Scope: [`crm`](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/scopes/permissions.html)\n> \n> Who can subscribe: any user\nThe `ONCRMLEADUPDATE` event will trigger when a lead is updated.\nEvents will not be sent to the application until the installation is complete. [Check the application installation](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/settings/app-installation/installation-finish.html)\n[What the handler receives](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/crm/leads/events/on-crm-lead-update.html#what-the-handler-receives)What the handler receives\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\nData is sent as a POST request\n ```\n{\n    \"event\": \"ONCRMLEADUPDATE\",\n    \"event_handler_id\": \"709\",\n    \"data\": {\n        \"FIELDS\": {\n            \"ID\": \"1000983\"\n        }\n    },\n    \"ts\": \"1742807189\",\n    \"auth\": {\n        \"access_token\": \"s6p6eclrvim6da22ft9ch94ekreb52lv\",\n        \"expires_in\": \"3600\",\n        \"scope\": \"crm\",\n        \"domain\": \"some-domain.bitrix24.com\",\n        \"server_endpoint\": \"https://oauth.bitrix.info/rest/\",\n        \"status\": \"L\",\n        \"client_endpoint\": \"https://some-domain.bitrix24.com/rest/\",\n        \"member_id\": \"a223c6b3710f85df22e9377d6c4f7553\",\n        \"refresh_token\": \"4s386p3q0tr8dy89xvmt96234v3dljg8\",\n        \"application_token\": \"51856fefc120afa4b628cc82d3935cce\"\n    }\n}\n```\n          \n| **Parameter**   `type` | **Description** |\n|---|---|\n| **event**   [`string`](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/data-types.html) | Symbolic code of the event.  In this case — `ONCRMLEADUPDATE` |\n| **event\\_handler\\_id**   [`integer`](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/data-types.html) | Identifier of the event handler |\n| **data**   [`object`](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/data-types.html) | Object containing information about the updated lead.  Contains a single key `FIELDS` |\n| **data.FIELDS**   [`object`](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/data-types.html) | Object containing information about the fields of the updated lead.  The structure is described [below](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/crm/leads/events/on-crm-lead-update.html#fields) |\n| **ts**   [`timestamp`](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/data-types.html) | Date and time the event was sent from the [event queue](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/events/index.html) |\n| **auth**   [`object`](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/data-types.html) | Object containing authorization parameters and information about the account where the event occurred. {"result":"# Overview of Events When Working with Leads
URL: https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/crm/leads/events/index.html

## Description
Events allow applications to respond to changes in almost real-time by receiving notifications about the creation, update, or deletion of leads.

## Content
Events allow applications to respond to changes in almost real-time: receiving notifications about the creation, update, or deletion of leads.
Detailed information on working with events is described in the article [Concept and Benefits of Event Processing](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/events/index.html).
> Quick navigation: [all events](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/crm/leads/events/index.html#all-events)
[How to Receive Events](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/crm/leads/events/index.html#how-to-receive-events)How to Receive Events
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
You can subscribe to lead events through:
- [outgoing webhook](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/local-integrations/local-webhooks.html)
- [application](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/settings/app-installation/index.html) and the method [event.bind](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/events/event-bind.html)
An example of a handler code for the event is described in the article [How to Test Your Handler for Processing Bitrix24 Events](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/events/test-handler.html).
[Server Availability for Sending and Receiving Events](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/crm/leads/events/index.html#server-availability-for-sending-and-receiving-events)Server Availability for Sending and Receiving Events
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The event handler server must be accessible from the outside. To configure the accessibility of the event handler server and application, refer to the article [Required network access](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com//settings/cloud-and-on-premise/network-access.html).
For cloud Bitrix24, no additional configuration is required. For on-premise Bitrix24, configure accessibility on the server or hosting according to the article [Required network access](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com//settings/cloud-and-on-premise/network-access.html).
[Overview of Events](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/crm/leads/events/index.html#all-events)Overview of Events
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
> Scope: [`crm`](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/scopes/permissions.html)
> 
> Who can subscribe: any user
| **Event** | **Triggered By** |
|---|---|
| [onCrmLeadAdd](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/crm/leads/events/on-crm-lead-add.html) | When a lead is added manually or via the [crm.lead.add](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/crm/leads/crm-lead-add.html) method |
| [onCrmLeadUpdate](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/crm/leads/events/on-crm-lead-update.html) | When a lead is updated manually or via the [crm.lead.update](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/crm/leads/crm-lead-update.html) method |
| [onCrmLeadDelete](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/crm/leads/events/on-crm-lead-delete.html) | When a lead is deleted manually or via the [crm.lead.delete](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/crm/leads/crm-lead-delete.html) method |"}

{"file_path": "/mnt/vdb/djc/work_djc/interactive_data_generation-master_multi_domain_new_struct/workspace/b24-mcp-dev--exaltedtrou6_claude_code_739/lead_qualification_workflow_config.json", "content": "# Bitrix24 Lead Qualification Automation Configuration

## Overview
This document provides the technical configuration for implementing an automated lead qualification workflow that identifies high-value leads ($10,000+) sitting in 'NEW' status for more than 7 days and escalates them to 'IN_PROCESS' status.

---

## 1. API Method: Query Leads by Status and Opportunity Amount

### Method: `crm.lead.list`

**Description:** Returns a list of leads based on filter criteria.

**Endpoint:** `POST /rest/crm.lead.list`

**Parameters:**
| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| order | object | No | Sorting order (e.g., `{ "DATE_CREATE": "ASC" }`) |
| filter | object | No | Filter criteria for leads |
| select | array | No | Fields to return |
| start | integer | No | Pagination offset |

**Sample Request - Query High-Value NEW Leads:**
```json
{
  "order": { "DATE_CREATE": "ASC" },
  "filter": {
    "STATUS_ID": "NEW",
    ">=OPPORTUNITY": "10000",
    "<DATE_CREATE": "2026-04-12"
  },
  "select": [
    "ID",
    "TITLE",
    "STATUS_ID",
    "OPPORTUNITY",
    "CURRENCY_ID",
    "DATE_CREATE",
    "ASSIGNED_BY_ID",
    "NAME",
    "LAST_NAME",
    "EMAIL",
    "PHONE"
  ]
}
```

**Key Filter Operators:**
- `>=OPPORTUNITY` - Greater than or equal to opportunity amount
- `<DATE_CREATE` - Leads created before this date (for 7+ days filter)
- `STATUS_ID` - Exact match on status ID

**Example cURL:**
```bash
curl -X POST \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'Accept: application/json' \
  -d '{
    "order": {"DATE_CREATE": "ASC"},
    "filter": {
      "STATUS_ID": "NEW",
      ">=OPPORTUNITY": "10000",
      "<DATE_CREATE": "2026-04-12"
    },
    "select": ["ID", "TITLE", "STATUS_ID", "OPPORTUNITY", "DATE_CREATE", "ASSIGNED_BY_ID"]
  }' \
  'https://your-bitrix24.com/rest/1/your-webhook/crm.lead.list'
```

---

## 2. Fields Required to Update Lead Status

### Method: `crm.lead.update`

**Description:** Updates an existing lead record.

**Endpoint:** `POST /rest/crm.lead.update`

**Parameters:**
| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| id | integer | **Yes** | Lead ID to update |
| fields | object | **Yes** | Object containing fields to update |
| params | object | No | Additional parameters (e.g., registerSoniaEvent) |

**Critical Fields for Status Update:**
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| STATUS_ID | string | Status identifier (e.g., "NEW", "IN_PROCESS", "JUNK") |
| STATUS_SEMANTIC_ID | string | Semantic identifier (P=process, S=success, F=failure) |
| ASSIGNED_BY_ID | integer | ID of the user assigned to the lead |
| COMMENTS | string | Comments about the status change (optional but recommended) |

---

## 3. Valid Lead Status IDs

### Method: `crm.status.list`

**Description:** Returns available status stages for leads.

**Endpoint:** `POST /rest/crm.status.list`

**To retrieve lead statuses:**
```json
{
  "order": { "SORT": "ASC" },
  "filter": { "ENTITY_ID": "STATUS" }
}
```

**Standard Lead Status IDs:**
| STATUS_ID | Name | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| NEW | New | Newly created lead |
| IN_PROCESS | In Progress | Lead being worked on |
| PROCESSED | Processed | Lead has been processed |
| JUNK | Junk | Unqualified/junk lead |
| CONVERTED | Converted | Lead converted to deal/contact |

**Status Semantics:**
- `process` - Active workflow (NEW, IN_PROCESS, PROCESSED)
- `success` - Successfully completed (CONVERTED)
- `failure` - Unsuccessful (JUNK)

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "result": [
    {
      "ID": "101",
      "ENTITY_ID": "STATUS",
      "STATUS_ID": "NEW",
      "NAME": "New",
      "SORT": "10",
      "SYSTEM": "Y",
      "COLOR": "#39A8EF",
      "SEMANTICS": null,
      "EXTRA": { "SEMANTICS": "process", "COLOR": "#39A8EF" }
    },
    {
      "ID": "102",
      "ENTITY_ID": "STATUS",
      "STATUS_ID": "IN_PROCESS",
      "NAME": "In Progress",
      "SORT": "20",
      "SYSTEM": "N",
      "COLOR": "#2FC6F6",
      "EXTRA": { "SEMANTICS": "process", "COLOR": "#2FC6F6" }
    }
  ],
  "total": 2
}
```

---

## 4. Sample JSON Payload for Updating Lead to IN_PROCESS

### Update Request:
```json
{
  "id": 12345,
  "fields": {
    "STATUS_ID": "IN_PROCESS",
    "COMMENTS": "Automated escalation: High-value lead (>$10,000) exceeded 7-day threshold in NEW status. Assigned for immediate follow-up."
  },
  "params": {
    "registerSoniaEvent": true
  }
}
```

### Example cURL:
```bash
curl -X POST \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'Accept: application/json' \
  -d '{
    "id": 12345,
    "fields": {
      "STATUS_ID": "IN_PROCESS",
      "COMMENTS": "Automated escalation: High-value lead ($15,000) exceeded 7-day threshold. Escalated for immediate follow-up."
    },
    "params": {
      "registerSoniaEvent": true
    }
  }' \
  'https://your-bitrix24.com/rest/1/your-webhook/crm.lead.update'
```

### Expected Response:
```json
{
  "result": true,
  "time": {
    "start": 1713525600.123456,
    "finish": 1713525600.234567,
    "duration": 0.111111,
    "processing": 0.055555,
    "date_start": "2026-04-19T12:00:00+00:00",
    "date_finish": "2026-04-19T12:00:00+00:00"
  }
}
```

---

## 5. Event: onCrmLeadUpdate

### Event Overview
The `onCrmLeadUpdate` event triggers when a lead is updated manually or via the `crm.lead.update` method.

**Event Name:** `onCrmLeadUpdate`

**Scope Required:** `crm`

**Triggered By:**
- Manual lead updates in Bitrix24 CRM
- API calls to `crm.lead.update`

### Subscribing to Events

**Method:** `event.bind`

**Request:**
```json
{
  "event": "onCrmLeadUpdate",
  "handler": "https://your-server.com/webhooks/lead-updated"
}
```

### Event Payload Structure:
```json
{
  "event": "onCrmLeadUpdate",
  "data": {
    "FIELDS": {
      "ID": "12345",
      "STATUS_ID": "IN_PROCESS"
    }
  },
  "auth": {
    "access_token": "your_access_token",
    "expires_in": "3600",
    "user_id": "1"
  }
}
```

### Event Handling Options:
1. **Outgoing Webhook** - Configure in Bitrix24 settings for simple integrations
2. **Application with event.bind** - For programmatic subscription

---

## 6. Lead Fields Reference

### Key Fields for Lead Management:
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| ID | integer | Unique lead identifier |
| TITLE | string | Lead title/name |
| STATUS_ID | string | Current status (NEW, IN_PROCESS, etc.) |
| OPPORTUNITY | double | Opportunity amount (numeric) |
| CURRENCY_ID | string | Currency code (USD, EUR, etc.) |
| DATE_CREATE | datetime | Creation timestamp |
| DATE_MODIFY | datetime | Last modification timestamp |
| ASSIGNED_BY_ID | integer | Assigned user ID |
| CREATED_BY_ID | integer | Creator user ID |
| COMMENTS | string | Comments/notes |

---

## Configuration Summary

| Setting | Value |
|---------|-------|
| Opportunity Threshold | $10,000 |
| Status Threshold | NEW |
| Time Threshold | 7 days |
| Target Status | IN_PROCESS |
| Required Scope | crm |
| Event Trigger | onCrmLeadUpdate |

---

## API Reference Links

- [crm.lead.list](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/crm/leads/crm-lead-list.html)
- [crm.lead.update](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/crm/leads/crm-lead-update.html)
- [crm.lead.fields](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/crm/leads/crm-lead-fields.html)
- [crm.status.list](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/crm/status/crm-status-list.html)
- [onCrmLeadUpdate Event](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/crm/leads/events/on-crm-lead-update.html)
"}

{"file_path": "/mnt/vdb/djc/work_djc/interactive_data_generation-master_multi_domain_new_struct/workspace/b24-mcp-dev--exaltedtrou6_claude_code_739/lead_qualification_workflow_analysis.md", "content": "# Lead Qualification Automation Workflow Analysis

## Business Problem
High-value leads (opportunity amount > $10,000) are sitting in 'NEW' status for more than 7 days without proper follow-up, resulting in lost sales opportunities and revenue.

---

## Workflow Architecture

### Phase 1: Lead Identification (Query)

**Trigger:** Scheduled job running daily (or more frequently)

**Steps:**
1. Calculate the date threshold (current date - 7 days)
2. Query Bitrix24 CRM using `crm.lead.list` with filters:
   - `STATUS_ID`: "NEW"
   - `>=OPPORTUNITY`: 10000
   - `<DATE_CREATE`: [calculated threshold date]
3. Capture all matching lead IDs and metadata

**Implementation Code Pattern:**
```
POST /rest/crm.lead.list
{
  "filter": {
    "STATUS_ID": "NEW",
    ">=OPPORTUNITY": "10000",
    "<DATE_CREATE": "NOW - 7 DAYS"
  },
  "select": ["ID", "TITLE", "OPPORTUNITY", "ASSIGNED_BY_ID", "EMAIL", "PHONE"]
}
```

---

### Phase 2: Lead Escalation (Update)

**Action:** Update each qualifying lead

**Steps:**
1. For each lead returned from Phase 1:
   - Update status using `crm.lead.update`
   - Set `STATUS_ID` to "IN_PROCESS"
   - Add automated comment explaining escalation
2. Optionally reassign to a senior sales representative

**Implementation Code Pattern:**
```
POST /rest/crm.lead.update
{
  "id": {LEAD_ID},
  "fields": {
    "STATUS_ID": "IN_PROCESS",
    "COMMENTS": "Auto-escalated: High-value lead exceeded 7-day follow-up threshold"
  }
}
```

---

### Phase 3: Notification (Optional Enhancement)

**Actions:**
1. Send notification to sales manager
2. Create activity/task assigned to sales rep
3. Log action in CRM timeline

---

## Event-Driven Enhancement: Using onCrmLeadUpdate

### Purpose
The `onCrmLeadUpdate` event enables real-time monitoring of lead changes, allowing for:

1. **Audit Trail** - Track when leads move between statuses
2. **Validation Rules** - Prevent status regression (e.g., IN_PROCESS back to NEW)
3. **Cascading Actions** - Trigger notifications or create activities when status changes
4. **SLA Monitoring** - Start/stop timers based on status transitions

### Event Subscription

**Setup via event.bind:**
```json
{
  "event": "onCrmLeadUpdate",
  "handler": "https://your-server.com/webhooks/lead-status-handler"
}
```

### Handler Workflow

When `onCrmLeadUpdate` fires:

1. **Parse Event Payload**
   - Extract lead ID and new STATUS_ID
   - Retrieve full lead details via `crm.lead.get`

2. **Evaluate Business Rules**
   - Is this a high-value lead (OPPORTUNITY >= $10,000)?
   - Is status transitioning away from NEW?
   - Is status staying in NEW beyond acceptable timeframe?

3. **Execute Actions**
   - If criteria met, trigger escalation workflow
   - If criteria not met, log for reporting

### Event Handler Pseudocode
```
function handleLeadUpdate(event):
    lead_id = event.data.FIELDS.ID

    // Get full lead details
    lead = crm.lead.get(lead_id)

    // Check if high-value lead moved to IN_PROCESS
    if lead.OPPORTUNITY >= 10000 AND lead.STATUS_ID == "IN_PROCESS":
        // Create follow-up activity
        crm.activity.add({
            "OWNER_ID": lead_id,
            "OWNER_TYPE_ID": 1,  // Lead
            "TYPE_ID": 2,  // Meeting
            "SUBJECT": "Follow up on high-value lead -$" + lead.OPPORTUNITY,
            "DEADLINE": NOW + 24 hours,
            "ASSIGNED_BY_ID": lead.ASSIGNED_BY_ID
        })

        // Notify sales manager
        send_notification(
            to: sales_manager,
            message: f"High-value lead {lead_id} ({lead.OPPORTUNITY}) escalated to IN_PROCESS"
        )
```

---

## Complete Workflow Diagram

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    SCHEDULED JOB (Daily)                        │
│                                                                 │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │  PHASE 1: Query Leads                                    │   │
│  │  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │   │
│  │  crm.lead.list                                          │   │
│  │  ├── filter: STATUS_ID = "NEW"                          │   │
│  │  ├── filter: OPPORTUNITY >= $10,000                     │   │
│  │  └── filter: DATE_CREATE < (NOW - 7 days)               │   │
│  └────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                           │                                     │
│                           ▼                                     │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │  PHASE 2: Process Each Lead                              │   │
│  │  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │   │
│  │  For each qualifying lead:                               │   │
│  │  ├── crm.lead.update(id, {STATUS_ID: "IN_PROCESS"})    │   │
│  │  └── Add escalatory comments                             │   │
│  └────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                           │                                     │
│                           ▼                                     │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │  PHASE 3: Notifications                                  │   │
│  │  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │   │
│  │  ├── Send email to sales manager                         │   │
│  │  ├── Create follow-up activity                           │   │
│  │  └── Log action for audit                                │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    REAL-TIME EVENT HANDLER                      │
│                                                                 │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │  onCrmLeadUpdate Subscription                            │   │
│  │  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │   │
│  │  event.bind(event: "onCrmLeadUpdate", handler: URL)     │   │
│  └────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                           │                                     │
│                           ▼                                     │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │  Event Received → Validate & Act                         │   │
│  │  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │   │
│  │  ├── Get lead details (crm.lead.get)                    │   │
│  │  ├── Check opportunity value                            │   │
│  │  └── Create follow-up activity for high-value leads     │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Implementation Recommendations

### 1. Scheduled Job Approach (Primary)
- **Frequency:** Run every 6-24 hours
- **Pros:** Simple to implement, reliable, batch processing
- **Cons:** Not real-time, may miss leads within the polling window

### 2. Event-Driven Approach (Enhancement)
- **Trigger:** `onCrmLeadUpdate` event subscription
- **Pros:** Real-time, immediate action on status changes
- **Cons:** Requires publicly accessible webhook endpoint, more complex setup

### 3. Hybrid Approach (Recommended)
- Use **scheduled job** for daily batch processing of stagnant leads
- Use **event handler** for real-time monitoring and activity creation
- Provides both proactive escalation and reactive monitoring

---

## Error Handling

| Error Code | Description | Recommended Action |
|------------|-------------|-------------------|
| 400 | Invalid parameters | Log error, validate filter syntax |
| 403 | Access denied | Check API permissions, verify scope |
| 404 | Lead not found | Lead may have been deleted, skip |
| 500 | Internal server error | Retry with exponential backoff |
| 503 | Query limit exceeded | Wait and retry, implement rate limiting |

---

## Success Metrics

| Metric | Target | Measurement |
|--------|--------|-------------|
| High-value lead response time | < 24 hours | Time from creation to IN_PROCESS |
| Escalation accuracy | 100% | Correct leads identified |
| False positives | 0% | Leads incorrectly escalated |
| Follow-up completion rate | 95% | Leads receiving follow-up activities |

---

## Summary

This workflow automates the identification and escalation of high-value leads that have been neglected. The primary mechanism uses `crm.lead.list` to query for leads meeting the criteria (NEW status, OPPORTUNITY >= $10,000, creation date > 7 days ago), then updates them to IN_PROCESS status via `crm.lead.update`. The `onCrmLeadUpdate` event provides an additional layer of real-time monitoring and can trigger follow-up activities when lead statuses change.

**Key API Methods:**
1. `crm.lead.list` - Query leads by filter criteria
2. `crm.lead.update` - Update lead status
3. `crm.status.list` - Get valid status IDs
4. `event.bind` - Subscribe to lead update events

**Key Event:**
- `onCrmLeadUpdate` - Triggers when any lead is modified
"}

1→# Bitrix24 Lead Qualification Automation Configuration
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→This document provides the technical configuration for implementing an automated lead qualification workflow that identifies high-value leads ($10,000+) sitting in 'NEW' status for more than 7 days and escalates them to 'IN_PROCESS' status.
     5→
     6→---
     7→
     8→## 1. API Method: Query Leads by Status and Opportunity Amount
     9→
    10→### Method: `crm.lead.list`
    11→
    12→**Description:** Returns a list of leads based on filter criteria.
    13→
    14→**Endpoint:** `POST /rest/crm.lead.list`
    15→
    16→**Parameters:**
    17→| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
    18→|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
    19→| order | object | No | Sorting order (e.g., `{ "DATE_CREATE": "ASC" }`) |
    20→| filter | object | No | Filter criteria for leads |
    21→| select | array | No | Fields to return |
    22→| start | integer | No | Pagination offset |
    23→
    24→**Sample Request - Query High-Value NEW Leads:**
    25→```json
    26→{
    27→  "order": { "DATE_CREATE": "ASC" },
    28→  "filter": {
    29→    "STATUS_ID": "NEW",
    30→    ">=OPPORTUNITY": "10000",
    31→    "<DATE_CREATE": "2026-04-12"
    32→  },
    33→  "select": [
    34→    "ID",
    35→    "TITLE",
    36→    "STATUS_ID",
    37→    "OPPORTUNITY",
    38→    "CURRENCY_ID",
    39→    "DATE_CREATE",
    40→    "ASSIGNED_BY_ID",
    41→    "NAME",
    42→    "LAST_NAME",
    43→    "EMAIL",
    44→    "PHONE"
    45→  ]
    46→}
    47→```
    48→
    49→**Key Filter Operators:**
    50→- `>=OPPORTUNITY` - Greater than or equal to opportunity amount
    51→- `<DATE_CREATE` - Leads created before this date (for 7+ days filter)
    52→- `STATUS_ID` - Exact match on status ID
    53→
    54→**Example cURL:**
    55→```bash
    56→curl -X POST \
    57→  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    58→  -H 'Accept: application/json' \
    59→  -d '{
    60→    "order": {"DATE_CREATE": "ASC"},
    61→    "filter": {
    62→      "STATUS_ID": "NEW",
    63→      ">=OPPORTUNITY": "10000",
    64→      "<DATE_CREATE": "2026-04-12"
    65→    },
    66→    "select": ["ID", "TITLE", "STATUS_ID", "OPPORTUNITY", "DATE_CREATE", "ASSIGNED_BY_ID"]
    67→  }' \
    68→  'https://your-bitrix24.com/rest/1/your-webhook/crm.lead.list'
    69→```
    70→
    71→---
    72→
    73→## 2. Fields Required to Update Lead Status
    74→
    75→### Method: `crm.lead.update`
    76→
    77→**Description:** Updates an existing lead record.
    78→
    79→**Endpoint:** `POST /rest/crm.lead.update`
    80→
    81→**Parameters:**
    82→| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
    83→|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
    84→| id | integer | **Yes** | Lead ID to update |
    85→| fields | object | **Yes** | Object containing fields to update |
    86→| params | object | No | Additional parameters (e.g., registerSoniaEvent) |
    87→
    88→**Critical Fields for Status Update:**
    89→| Field | Type | Description |
    90→|-------|------|-------------|
    91→| STATUS_ID | string | Status identifier (e.g., "NEW", "IN_PROCESS", "JUNK") |
    92→| STATUS_SEMANTIC_ID | string | Semantic identifier (P=process, S=success, F=failure) |
    93→| ASSIGNED_BY_ID | integer | ID of the user assigned to the lead |
    94→| COMMENTS | string | Comments about the status change (optional but recommended) |
    95→
    96→---
    97→
    98→## 3. Valid Lead Status IDs
    99→
   100→### Method: `crm.status.list`
   101→
   102→**Description:** Returns available status stages for leads.
   103→
   104→**Endpoint:** `POST /rest/crm.status.list`
   105→
   106→**To retrieve lead statuses:**
   107→```json
   108→{
   109→  "order": { "SORT": "ASC" },
   110→  "filter": { "ENTITY_ID": "STATUS" }
   111→}
   112→```
   113→
   114→**Standard Lead Status IDs:**
   115→| STATUS_ID | Name | Description |
   116→|-----------|------|-------------|
   117→| NEW | New | Newly created lead |
   118→| IN_PROCESS | In Progress | Lead being worked on |
   119→| PROCESSED | Processed | Lead has been processed |
   120→| JUNK | Junk | Unqualified/junk lead |
   121→| CONVERTED | Converted | Lead converted to deal/contact |
   122→
   123→**Status Semantics:**
   124→- `process` - Active workflow (NEW, IN_PROCESS, PROCESSED)
   125→- `success` - Successfully completed (CONVERTED)
   126→- `failure` - Unsuccessful (JUNK)
   127→
   128→**Example Response:**
   129→```json
   130→{
   131→  "result": [
   132→    {
   133→      "ID": "101",
   134→      "ENTITY_ID": "STATUS",
   135→      "STATUS_ID": "NEW",
   136→      "NAME": "New",
   137→      "SORT": "10",
   138→      "SYSTEM": "Y",
   139→      "COLOR": "#39A8EF",
   140→      "SEMANTICS": null,
   141→      "EXTRA": { "SEMANTICS": "process", "COLOR": "#39A8EF" }
   142→    },
   143→    {
   144→      "ID": "102",
   145→      "ENTITY_ID": "STATUS",
   146→      "STATUS_ID": "IN_PROCESS",
   147→      "NAME": "In Progress",
   148→      "SORT": "20",
   149→      "SYSTEM": "N",
   150→      "COLOR": "#2FC6F6",
   151→      "EXTRA": { "SEMANTICS": "process", "COLOR": "#2FC6F6" }
   152→    }
   153→  ],
   154→  "total": 2
   155→}
   156→```
   157→
   158→---
   159→
   160→## 4. Sample JSON Payload for Updating Lead to IN_PROCESS
   161→
   162→### Update Request:
   163→```json
   164→{
   165→  "id": 12345,
   166→  "fields": {
   167→    "STATUS_ID": "IN_PROCESS",
   168→    "COMMENTS": "Automated escalation: High-value lead (>$10,000) exceeded 7-day threshold in NEW status. Assigned for immediate follow-up."
   169→  },
   170→  "params": {
   171→    "registerSoniaEvent": true
   172→  }
   173→}
   174→```
   175→
   176→### Example cURL:
   177→```bash
   178→curl -X POST \
   179→  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
   180→  -H 'Accept: application/json' \
   181→  -d '{
   182→    "id": 12345,
   183→    "fields": {
   184→      "STATUS_ID": "IN_PROCESS",
   185→      "COMMENTS": "Automated escalation: High-value lead ($15,000) exceeded 7-day threshold. Escalated for immediate follow-up."
   186→    },
   187→    "params": {
   188→      "registerSoniaEvent": true
   189→    }
   190→  }' \
   191→  'https://your-bitrix24.com/rest/1/your-webhook/crm.lead.update'
   192→```
   193→
   194→### Expected Response:
   195→```json
   196→{
   197→  "result": true,
   198→  "time": {
   199→    "start": 1713525600.123456,
   200→    "finish": 1713525600.234567,
   201→    "duration": 0.111111,
   202→    "processing": 0.055555,
   203→    "date_start": "2026-04-19T12:00:00+00:00",
   204→    "date_finish": "2026-04-19T12:00:00+00:00"
   205→  }
   206→}
   207→```
   208→
   209→---
   210→
   211→## 5. Event: onCrmLeadUpdate
   212→
   213→### Event Overview
   214→The `onCrmLeadUpdate` event triggers when a lead is updated manually or via the `crm.lead.update` method.
   215→
   216→**Event Name:** `onCrmLeadUpdate`
   217→
   218→**Scope Required:** `crm`
   219→
   220→**Triggered By:**
   221→- Manual lead updates in Bitrix24 CRM
   222→- API calls to `crm.lead.update`
   223→
   224→### Subscribing to Events
   225→
   226→**Method:** `event.bind`
   227→
   228→**Request:**
   229→```json
   230→{
   231→  "event": "onCrmLeadUpdate",
   232→  "handler": "https://your-server.com/webhooks/lead-updated"
   233→}
   234→```
   235→
   236→### Event Payload Structure:
   237→```json
   238→{
   239→  "event": "onCrmLeadUpdate",
   240→  "data": {
   241→    "FIELDS": {
   242→      "ID": "12345",
   243→      "STATUS_ID": "IN_PROCESS"
   244→    }
   245→  },
   246→  "auth": {
   247→    "access_token": "your_access_token",
   248→    "expires_in": "3600",
   249→    "user_id": "1"
   250→  }
   251→}
   252→```
   253→
   254→### Event Handling Options:
   255→1. **Outgoing Webhook** - Configure in Bitrix24 settings for simple integrations
   256→2. **Application with event.bind** - For programmatic subscription
   257→
   258→---
   259→
   260→## 6. Lead Fields Reference
   261→
   262→### Key Fields for Lead Management:
   263→| Field | Type | Description |
   264→|-------|------|-------------|
   265→| ID | integer | Unique lead identifier |
   266→| TITLE | string | Lead title/name |
   267→| STATUS_ID | string | Current status (NEW, IN_PROCESS, etc.) |
   268→| OPPORTUNITY | double | Opportunity amount (numeric) |
   269→| CURRENCY_ID | string | Currency code (USD, EUR, etc.) |
   270→| DATE_CREATE | datetime | Creation timestamp |
   271→| DATE_MODIFY | datetime | Last modification timestamp |
   272→| ASSIGNED_BY_ID | integer | Assigned user ID |
   273→| CREATED_BY_ID | integer | Creator user ID |
   274→| COMMENTS | string | Comments/notes |
   275→
   276→---
   277→
   278→## Configuration Summary
   279→
   280→| Setting | Value |
   281→|---------|-------|
   282→| Opportunity Threshold | $10,000 |
   283→| Status Threshold | NEW |
   284→| Time Threshold | 7 days |
   285→| Target Status | IN_PROCESS |
   286→| Required Scope | crm |
   287→| Event Trigger | onCrmLeadUpdate |
   288→
   289→---
   290→
   291→## API Reference Links
   292→
   293→- [crm.lead.list](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/crm/leads/crm-lead-list.html)
   294→- [crm.lead.update](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/crm/leads/crm-lead-update.html)
   295→- [crm.lead.fields](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/crm/leads/crm-lead-fields.html)
   296→- [crm.status.list](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/crm/status/crm-status-list.html)
   297→- [onCrmLeadUpdate Event](https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/crm/leads/events/on-crm-lead-update.html)
   298→

1→# Lead Qualification Automation Workflow Analysis
     2→
     3→## Business Problem
     4→High-value leads (opportunity amount > $10,000) are sitting in 'NEW' status for more than 7 days without proper follow-up, resulting in lost sales opportunities and revenue.
     5→
     6→---
     7→
     8→## Workflow Architecture
     9→
    10→### Phase 1: Lead Identification (Query)
    11→
    12→**Trigger:** Scheduled job running daily (or more frequently)
    13→
    14→**Steps:**
    15→1. Calculate the date threshold (current date - 7 days)
    16→2. Query Bitrix24 CRM using `crm.lead.list` with filters:
    17→   - `STATUS_ID`: "NEW"
    18→   - `>=OPPORTUNITY`: 10000
    19→   - `<DATE_CREATE`: [calculated threshold date]
    20→3. Capture all matching lead IDs and metadata
    21→
    22→**Implementation Code Pattern:**
    23→```
    24→POST /rest/crm.lead.list
    25→{
    26→  "filter": {
    27→    "STATUS_ID": "NEW",
    28→    ">=OPPORTUNITY": "10000",
    29→    "<DATE_CREATE": "NOW - 7 DAYS"
    30→  },
    31→  "select": ["ID", "TITLE", "OPPORTUNITY", "ASSIGNED_BY_ID", "EMAIL", "PHONE"]
    32→}
    33→```
    34→
    35→---
    36→
    37→### Phase 2: Lead Escalation (Update)
    38→
    39→**Action:** Update each qualifying lead
    40→
    41→**Steps:**
    42→1. For each lead returned from Phase 1:
    43→   - Update status using `crm.lead.update`
    44→   - Set `STATUS_ID` to "IN_PROCESS"
    45→   - Add automated comment explaining escalation
    46→2. Optionally reassign to a senior sales representative
    47→
    48→**Implementation Code Pattern:**
    49→```
    50→POST /rest/crm.lead.update
    51→{
    52→  "id": {LEAD_ID},
    53→  "fields": {
    54→    "STATUS_ID": "IN_PROCESS",
    55→    "COMMENTS": "Auto-escalated: High-value lead exceeded 7-day follow-up threshold"
    56→  }
    57→}
    58→```
    59→
    60→---
    61→
    62→### Phase 3: Notification (Optional Enhancement)
    63→
    64→**Actions:**
    65→1. Send notification to sales manager
    66→2. Create activity/task assigned to sales rep
    67→3. Log action in CRM timeline
    68→
    69→---
    70→
    71→## Event-Driven Enhancement: Using onCrmLeadUpdate
    72→
    73→### Purpose
    74→The `onCrmLeadUpdate` event enables real-time monitoring of lead changes, allowing for:
    75→
    76→1. **Audit Trail** - Track when leads move between statuses
    77→2. **Validation Rules** - Prevent status regression (e.g., IN_PROCESS back to NEW)
    78→3. **Cascading Actions** - Trigger notifications or create activities when status changes
    79→4. **SLA Monitoring** - Start/stop timers based on status transitions
    80→
    81→### Event Subscription
    82→
    83→**Setup via event.bind:**
    84→```json
    85→{
    86→  "event": "onCrmLeadUpdate",
    87→  "handler": "https://your-server.com/webhooks/lead-status-handler"
    88→}
    89→```
    90→
    91→### Handler Workflow
    92→
    93→When `onCrmLeadUpdate` fires:
    94→
    95→1. **Parse Event Payload**
    96→   - Extract lead ID and new STATUS_ID
    97→   - Retrieve full lead details via `crm.lead.get`
    98→
    99→2. **Evaluate Business Rules**
   100→   - Is this a high-value lead (OPPORTUNITY >= $10,000)?
   101→   - Is status transitioning away from NEW?
   102→   - Is status staying in NEW beyond acceptable timeframe?
   103→
   104→3. **Execute Actions**
   105→   - If criteria met, trigger escalation workflow
   106→   - If criteria not met, log for reporting
   107→
   108→### Event Handler Pseudocode
   109→```
   110→function handleLeadUpdate(event):
   111→    lead_id = event.data.FIELDS.ID
   112→
   113→    // Get full lead details
   114→    lead = crm.lead.get(lead_id)
   115→
   116→    // Check if high-value lead moved to IN_PROCESS
   117→    if lead.OPPORTUNITY >= 10000 AND lead.STATUS_ID == "IN_PROCESS":
   118→        // Create follow-up activity
   119→        crm.activity.add({
   120→            "OWNER_ID": lead_id,
   121→            "OWNER_TYPE_ID": 1,  // Lead
   122→            "TYPE_ID": 2,  // Meeting
   123→            "SUBJECT": "Follow up on high-value lead -$" + lead.OPPORTUNITY,
   124→            "DEADLINE": NOW + 24 hours,
   125→            "ASSIGNED_BY_ID": lead.ASSIGNED_BY_ID
   126→        })
   127→
   128→        // Notify sales manager
   129→        send_notification(
   130→            to: sales_manager,
   131→            message: f"High-value lead {lead_id} ({lead.OPPORTUNITY}) escalated to IN_PROCESS"
   132→        )
   133→```
   134→
   135→---
   136→
   137→## Complete Workflow Diagram
   138→
   139→```
   140→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   141→│                    SCHEDULED JOB (Daily)                        │
   142→│                                                                 │
   143→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
   144→│  │  PHASE 1: Query Leads                                    │   │
   145→│  │  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │   │
   146→│  │  crm.lead.list                                          │   │
   147→│  │  ├── filter: STATUS_ID = "NEW"                          │   │
   148→│  │  ├── filter: OPPORTUNITY >= $10,000                     │   │
   149→│  │  └── filter: DATE_CREATE < (NOW - 7 days)               │   │
   150→│  └────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────┘   │
   151→│                           │                                     │
   152→│                           ▼                                     │
   153→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
   154→│  │  PHASE 2: Process Each Lead                              │   │
   155→│  │  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │   │
   156→│  │  For each qualifying lead:                               │   │
   157→│  │  ├── crm.lead.update(id, {STATUS_ID: "IN_PROCESS"})    │   │
   158→│  │  └── Add escalatory comments                             │   │
   159→│  └────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────┘   │
   160→│                           │                                     │
   161→│                           ▼                                     │
   162→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
   163→│  │  PHASE 3: Notifications                                  │   │
   164→│  │  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │   │
   165→│  │  ├── Send email to sales manager                         │   │
   166→│  │  ├── Create follow-up activity                           │   │
   167→│  │  └── Log action for audit                                │   │
   168→│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
   169→│                                                                 │
   170→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   171→
   172→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   173→│                    REAL-TIME EVENT HANDLER                      │
   174→│                                                                 │
   175→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
   176→│  │  onCrmLeadUpdate Subscription                            │   │
   177→│  │  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │   │
   178→│  │  event.bind(event: "onCrmLeadUpdate", handler: URL)     │   │
   179→│  └────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────┘   │
   180→│                           │                                     │
   181→│                           ▼                                     │
   182→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
   183→│  │  Event Received → Validate & Act                         │   │
   184→│  │  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │   │
   185→│  │  ├── Get lead details (crm.lead.get)                    │   │
   186→│  │  ├── Check opportunity value                            │   │
   187→│  │  └── Create follow-up activity for high-value leads     │   │
   188→│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
   189→│                                                                 │
   190→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   191→```
   192→
   193→---
   194→
   195→## Implementation Recommendations
   196→
   197→### 1. Scheduled Job Approach (Primary)
   198→- **Frequency:** Run every 6-24 hours
   199→- **Pros:** Simple to implement, reliable, batch processing
   200→- **Cons:** Not real-time, may miss leads within the polling window
   201→
   202→### 2. Event-Driven Approach (Enhancement)
   203→- **Trigger:** `onCrmLeadUpdate` event subscription
   204→- **Pros:** Real-time, immediate action on status changes
   205→- **Cons:** Requires publicly accessible webhook endpoint, more complex setup
   206→
   207→### 3. Hybrid Approach (Recommended)
   208→- Use **scheduled job** for daily batch processing of stagnant leads
   209→- Use **event handler** for real-time monitoring and activity creation
   210→- Provides both proactive escalation and reactive monitoring
   211→
   212→---
   213→
   214→## Error Handling
   215→
   216→| Error Code | Description | Recommended Action |
   217→|------------|-------------|-------------------|
   218→| 400 | Invalid parameters | Log error, validate filter syntax |
   219→| 403 | Access denied | Check API permissions, verify scope |
   220→| 404 | Lead not found | Lead may have been deleted, skip |
   221→| 500 | Internal server error | Retry with exponential backoff |
   222→| 503 | Query limit exceeded | Wait and retry, implement rate limiting |
   223→
   224→---
   225→
   226→## Success Metrics
   227→
   228→| Metric | Target | Measurement |
   229→|--------|--------|-------------|
   230→| High-value lead response time | < 24 hours | Time from creation to IN_PROCESS |
   231→| Escalation accuracy | 100% | Correct leads identified |
   232→| False positives | 0% | Leads incorrectly escalated |
   233→| Follow-up completion rate | 95% | Leads receiving follow-up activities |
   234→
   235→---
   236→
   237→## Summary
   238→
   239→This workflow automates the identification and escalation of high-value leads that have been neglected. The primary mechanism uses `crm.lead.list` to query for leads meeting the criteria (NEW status, OPPORTUNITY >= $10,000, creation date > 7 days ago), then updates them to IN_PROCESS status via `crm.lead.update`. The `onCrmLeadUpdate` event provides an additional layer of real-time monitoring and can trigger follow-up activities when lead statuses change.
   240→
   241→**Key API Methods:**
   242→1. `crm.lead.list` - Query leads by filter criteria
   243→2. `crm.lead.update` - Update lead status
   244→3. `crm.status.list` - Get valid status IDs
   245→4. `event.bind` - Subscribe to lead update events
   246→
   247→**Key Event:**
   248→- `onCrmLeadUpdate` - Triggers when any lead is modified
   249→

function.name: Agent
function.description: Launch a new agent to handle complex, multi-step tasks autonomously. The Agent tool launches specialized agents (subprocesses) that autonomously handle complex tasks. Each agent type has specific capabilities and tools available to it. Available agent types and the tools they have access to: - general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *) - statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit) - Explore: Fast agent specialized for exploring codebases. Use this when you need to quickly find files by patterns (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), search code for keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer questions about the codebase (eg. "how do API endpoints work?"). When calling this agent, specify the desired thoroughness level: "quick" for basic searches, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" for comprehensive analysis across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit) - Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit) When using the Agent tool, specify a subagent_type parameter to select which agent type to use. If omitted, the general-purpose agent is used. When NOT to use the Agent tool: - If you want to read a specific file path, use the Read tool or the Glob tool instead of the Agent tool, to find the match more quickly - If you are searching for a specific class definition like "class Foo", use the Glob tool instead, to find the match more quickly - If you are searching for code within a specific file or set of 2-3 files, use the Read tool instead of the Agent tool, to find the match more quickly - Other tasks that are not related to the agent descriptions above Usage notes: - Always include a short description (3-5 words) summarizing what the agent will do - Launch multiple agents concurrently whenever possible, to maximize performance; to do that, use a single message with multiple tool uses - When the agent is done, it will return a single message back to you. The result returned by the agent is not visible to the user. To show the user the result, you should send a text message back to the user with a concise summary of the result. - You can optionally run agents in the background using the run_in_background parameter. When an agent runs in the background, you will be automatically notified when it completes — do NOT sleep, poll, or proactively check on its progress. Continue with other work or respond to the user instead. - **Foreground vs background**: Use foreground (default) when you need the agent's results before you can proceed — e.g., research agents whose findings inform your next steps. Use background when you have genuinely independent work to do in parallel. - To continue a previously spawned agent, use SendMessage with the agent's ID or name as the `to` field. The agent resumes with its full context preserved. Each Agent invocation starts fresh — provide a complete task description. - Provide clear, detailed prompts so the agent can work autonomously and return exactly the information you need. - The agent's outputs should generally be trusted - Clearly tell the agent whether you expect it to write code or just to do research (search, file reads, web fetches, etc.), since it is not aware of the user's intent - If the agent description mentions that it should be used proactively, then you should try your best to use it without the user having to ask for it first. Use your judgement. - If the user specifies that they want you to run agents "in parallel", you MUST send a single message with multiple Agent tool use content blocks. For example, if you need to launch both a build-validator agent and a test-runner agent in parallel, send a single message with both tool calls. - You can optionally set `isolation: "worktree"` to run the agent in a temporary git worktree, giving it an isolated copy of the repository. The worktree is automatically cleaned up if the agent makes no changes; if changes are made, the worktree path and branch are returned in the result. Example usage: "test-runner": use this agent after you are done writing code to run tests "greeting-responder": use this agent to respond to user greetings with a friendly joke user: "Please write a function that checks if a number is prime" assistant: I'm going to use the Write tool to write the following code: function isPrime(n) { if (n <= 1) return false for (let i = 2; i * i <= n; i++) { if (n % i === 0) return false } return true } Since a significant piece of code was written and the task was completed, now use the test-runner agent to run the tests assistant: Uses the Agent tool to launch the test-runner agent user: "Hello" Since the user is greeting, use the greeting-responder agent to respond with a friendly joke assistant: "I'm going to use the Agent tool to launch the greeting-responder agent"

description.description: A short (3-5 word) description of the task

prompt.description: The task for the agent to perform

model.description: Optional model override for this agent. Takes precedence over the agent definition's model frontmatter. If omitted, uses the agent definition's model, or inherits from the parent.

function.name: TaskOutput
